paper.title_en: Gauge-independent approach to inflation in quadratic gravity
paper.abstract_en: We investigate the scalar sector of linear cosmological perturbations in quadratic gravity. Working in the Einstein frame, we derive the equations of motion in a gauge-independent manner and express them in terms of three sets of gauge-invariant variables. This approach allows us to distinguish genuine physical effects from gauge artefacts, which is particularly relevant for assessing the stability of perturbations in this theory. In the superhorizon limit, we obtain the leading-order behaviour of the relevant gauge-invariant variables and analyse the perturbations in commonly used gauges. We find that the Newtonian gauge exhibits an apparent instability, characterised by the exponential growth of the metric perturbations. However, this growth is non-generic and gauge-dependent; in the other gauges analysed in this work, the perturbations remain well behaved within the perturbative regime. Our analysis also demonstrates how the evolution behaviour of a gauge-invariant variable changes under the frame transformation and clarifies the relation between results obtained in the Jordan and Einstein frames.
